question_text: Describe what happens to a spring when a force is applied that exceeds its elastic limit. What is the difference between elastic and inelastic deformation?
correct_answer: When force exceeds the elastic limit, the spring experiences inelastic deformation and will not return to its original shape when the force is removed. Elastic deformation is temporary—the spring returns to its original shape once the force is removed. Inelastic deformation is permanent—the spring remains permanently stretched or damaged.
explanation: The elastic limit is the maximum force a spring can withstand while still obeying Hooke's Law and returning to original shape. Beyond this, permanent damage occurs.
